item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sweet Christ that was gut punch. Equal parts horrifying and tragic. I feel like you should do some sarkic scips after reading that surgery log. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: very gross! what the fuck. 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember originally downvoting this page due to the image, which feels really excessive, a touch exploitative.

I worked around to an upvote due to how singularly grusome and gut-wrenching it is. It's abhorrent. It's ghastly and horrifically unsettling, and makes me deeply uncomfortable, and I've no choice but to rate positively that which evokes such a powerful reaction from me.

I do still think the inclusion of the photo undermines to some extent the pointedly visceral writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First off, nobody is obligated to not downvote your article simply because you are new. Moreover, we stress that new users should not immediately jump to posting an article. The site has an abundance of resources to avail yourself of in order to improve your articles before posting them. You have nobody to blame but yourself for not using them.

Secondly, it's totally bad form to comment on someone else's article to ask them to rescind a down vote on your own. It's totally off-topic and comes off as pleading and whiny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi.

Dog Teeth.

Dont downvote my page because its bad.
I joined 12 hours ago.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, but he says they can withstand a car crash. That was a very intentional analogy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is some pure utter horror; reminds me of the better made examples of "new French extremity". I honestly don't know how you managed to get this typed out, and I mean that as a compliment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you should find a good psychiatrist as soon as possible. I mean come on, deformed, self-mutilating, monster abortions described in an unimaginably hideous level of detail? It’s disturbing, it’s horrible, and it crosses about twenty lines in about twenty different ways. This is wrong. Maybe you wanted everyone to be shocked at how badly you could make a baby mutilate itself, but I’m coming out of this wondering if you’re a serial killer. I thought tabula rasa was bad, but this? NO.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Baby horror? Someone trying to step in on my turf?

Oh but this is quite a different direction. I admit that I wasn't affected, really, but I'm not easily disturbed. Still, +1 with gusto. You did body horror well. I did note one thing, though… these babies didn't feel anomalous until you said "they can withstand things normal babies can not". Which, like, feels like an afterthought. "Wait, these aren't anomalous yet, really, I have to make them anomalous." I noticed that as I read the article. But, anyways, this is good. Damn good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
<jjcooper0811> DogTeeth: god DAMN that is a hell of a picture
<DogTeeth> jjcooper0811 <3
<DogTeeth> it's real
<DogTeeth> it's a preserved specimen
<jjcooper0811> I DIDN'T NEED TO KNOW THAT

Take your upvote. I hope it was worth depriving me of the ability to sleep.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's a passage in a novel I read that includes the lyrics of a fictional song, "The Lonesome Love Song of the Sad Sewer Babies", that has long been my gold standard for baby horror.

A challenger has appeared. It uses Bloody Surgical Imagery. It's super effective!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hits that sweet spot between poignant and disturbing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Typos suck. Thanks so much for pointing them out! Fixed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That log… that log…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Super Goddamn Disturping. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, so I like this as much as I did in draft form, but I still think the story could have been a little more complex or ambiguous. It feels a tiny bit like a simple narrative tacked onto brilliantly horrific imagery, and that works to its detriment for me.

Regardless, this is horror done incredibly, sickeningly well. +1, but I feel sick doing it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is well written and certainly gruesome, but this kind of thing doesn't really have much of an effect on me; it always just feels like grossness for the sake of being gross. Obviously just a personal preference thing though - certainly seems like it achieves what it was trying to do! I did like the premise behind this doctors experimentation though, so that was definitely an interesting hook.

Couple of issues
– hide block

The following interview was interviewed

Couple uses of "interview" here read clunkily; I'd change the second to "performed".

Your Teletubbies toy is dated to 1988, but teletubbies didn't exist until 1997.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The sheer verisimilitude by which you described the surgery log is something out of nightmares. The article manages to humanize what has become dehumanized through unethical experimentation. For that, it serves as an interesting reminder of the fragility of the human body and the human mind.

Brilliant work, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: switched it up! thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this. At first I was thinking "oh, it's just magic dumpster babies", but the style was interesting enough to pull me to the end where you reveal that it's actually something else entirely.

One bit of feedback. This sentence:
Cooke County Landfill has since been abandoned following the company relocation, and as such, SCP-3486 have become reliant on Foundation personnel for anonymous deliveries of basic resources via garbage chutes.

Might read better as:
Cooke County Landfill has since been abandoned due to the managing company's relocation and, as such, SCP-3486 have become reliant on Foundation personnel for anonymous deliveries of basic resources via garbage chutes

Even if you don't take the reword, please fix the comma as I have since it's currently incorrect :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's certainly good horror as far as being nasty and filled with disturbing imagery goes, but it's also not subtle in the least and is teetering on the line of relying on shock value and being too over-the-top to take seriously. Novoted, though more out of personal taste than anything else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: confirmed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember critiquing this in the chatroom. It was pretty good to start with and it's had a shit ton of improvements since then.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dude's a furry, Ofcourse he isn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I honestly can't stomach the first collapsible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hot Hell.

This is tragic and disgusting and horrifying all at once, which is a good blend for a skip. That surgery log sure is… something, too, bud. You alright?

+1

item.title: I'm not sleeping for another three weeks.
item.content:encoded:
I remember when you first showed me this in our exchange. The idea was so unique yet so utterly horrifying that I was left speechless and quite frankly distraught, something of a rarity when reading drafts.

Now?
I'm fucking terrified.

A combination of Victorian horror and that god-awful surgery log combine in this article to form something straight out of the nightmares of a serial killer. The nice little endnote that they were all products of a woman trying to recreate her son? Fuck that. I'm not even considering closing my eyes in fear of seeing these horrendous abominations reattach plastic faces onto themselves.

Solid writing, a fantastic combination of styles and plot points, and just plain disgust really churned out a beautiful piece of work. Well done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Image credit.

baby horror
(thank you @ SCP Chat)
